# Zaileys

> Zaileys is a type-safe, batteries-included WhatsApp bot framework for Node.js and TypeScript, built on top of Baileys. It exposes a fluent message builder, interactive messages (buttons, lists, carousels), AIRich markdown responses, a command framework, broadcast and scheduling automation, media processing, typed domain modules (profile, chats, contacts, groups, newsletters, communities, business catalog), and pluggable storage adapters, with automatic reconnect and a clean event model.

Key facts an LLM should know:

- Package name on npm: `zaileys` (`npm install zaileys`). Imported as `import { Client } from 'zaileys'`. Current line: v4.4.x.
- Runtime support: Node.js 20+, Bun, Deno, and Termux (Android). Ships dual ESM/CJS builds.
- The entry point is the `Client` class. By default it auto-connects on construction (`autoConnect: true`); set `autoConnect: false` and call `await client.connect()` to control timing.
- Login methods: QR code (`authType: 'qr'`, default) or pairing code (`authType: 'pairing'` + `phoneNumber`).
- Receiving: typed events. `client.on('message', handler)` is the umbrella event that fires once for ANY inbound message type; per-type events (`text`, `image`, `video`, `audio`, `document`, `sticker`, `reaction`, `poll-vote`, `button-click`, `list-select`, `mention`, `mention-all`, group/call/newsletter, etc.) still fire. The handler gets a typed message context with `senderId`, `roomId`, `text`, `mentions` (resolved to phone-number JIDs), `staticId` (stable per room+sender, a good conversation key), `uniqueId`, `senderDevice`, content-derived flags, and `reply()` / `react()` / `replied()` / `media`.
- `chatType` values include `text`, `image`, `video`, `audio`, `document`, `sticker`, `poll`, `contact`, `location`, `live-location`, `event`, `album`, `group-invite`, `product`, `order`, `payment`, `buttons`, `list`, `interactive`, `template`. `ctx.media` is a discriminated union covering attachments plus poll/contact/location/event/album/group-invite/product/order/payment/link.
- Sending uses a fluent builder: `await client.send(jid).text('hi')` returns a `WAMessageKey`. Builders: text, image, video, videoNote (round PTV), audio, document, sticker, location, contact, poll, album, buttons, list, carousel, template, event, groupInvite, product, requestPhoneNumber, sharePhoneNumber, limitSharing. Note: WhatsApp renders `event()` only in groups (not 1:1 DMs); `groupInvite().expiresAt` is unix SECONDS.
- AIRich: pass plain markdown to `client.send(jid).text(markdown, { rich: true, title, footer, sources })` to render rich WhatsApp messages (bold, links, tables, LaTeX, code, images, and `:::suggest` / `:::tip` / `:::video` / `:::reels` / `:::post` / `:::product` directives). There is no separate `aiRich()` method.
- Mutations: `client.edit(key)`, `client.react(key, emoji)` (empty string removes), `client.delete(key, { forEveryone })`, `client.forward(key, jid)`, `client.pin(key, { duration })` / `client.unpin(key)`, `client.setDisappearing(jid, seconds)`. Async LID/PN helpers: `client.lidToPn(lid)` / `client.pnToLid(pn)`.
- Domain modules (lazy getters): `client.profile` (name/status/picture), `client.chat` (archive/pin/mute/markRead/star/delete/clear), `client.contact` (check/save/remove), `client.business` (catalog/collections/products), `client.group`, `client.newsletter`, `client.community`, `client.privacy`, `client.presence`.
- Commands: set `commandPrefix` in options, then `client.command('name', handler)` (aliases via `name|alias`), with `client.use(middleware)`.
- Plugins: drop files in a folder and load them with `plugins: { dir: './plugins' }`. Author with `export default definePlugin({ name, setup(ctx) { ... }, onUnload? })`; `ctx` can register `command`/`use`/`on`/`once` plus a returned teardown, all auto-reversed on unload. Recursive nested scan, hot-reload on by default (`watch: true`), error-isolated (one bad plugin is skipped). Plugins are trusted in-process code (no sandbox).
- Automation: `client.broadcast(recipients, builderFn, { rateLimitPerSec, onProgress })` and `client.scheduleAt(...)`.
- Storage adapters (auth + message stores): File (default), Memory, SQLite, Postgres, Redis, Convex. Optional peer deps: `better-sqlite3`, `pg`, `redis`, `convex`. Media processing uses bundled ffmpeg and optionally `sharp` (falls back to `jimp`).
- Auto-delete: old messages are pruned from the local store on a rolling window — enabled by default with a 1-month `maxAgeMs` (`autoDelete: false` disables; override `maxAgeMs`/`maxPerChat`/`intervalMs`/`chats`). Local store cleanup only — it never deletes messages on WhatsApp. Backed by optional `MessageStore.pruneMessages`/`deleteMessage`, implemented natively by all built-in adapters.
- Utilities exported from `zaileys`: JID helpers (`isJid`, `normalizeJid`, `isLidJid`, `isPnJid`, `jidToPhone`, `phoneToJid`, plus re-exported baileys `jidDecode`/`jidEncode`/`jidNormalizedUser`/`areJidsSameUser`/`isJidGroup`/`isJidNewsletter`/`isLidUser`/`isPnUser`), id hashers (`computeUniqueId`, `computeStaticId`), `extractLinks`, `senderDeviceOf`, `epochSecondsToMs`, `loadMedia`, `detectMimeFromBuffer`, `chunk`.
- Errors are typed classes with `.code`: `ZaileysBuilderError`, `ZaileysCommandError`, `ZaileysDomainError`, `ZaileysAutomationError`, `ZaileysStoreError`.
- License: MIT. Source: https://github.com/zeative/zaileys

## Docs

- [Introduction](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/): What Zaileys is and a hello-world bot
- [AI Skill](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/skill): Install the official Agent Skill (Claude Code plugin or npx skills)
- [Installation](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/installation): Install with npm/pnpm/yarn/bun, runtimes, peer deps
- [Getting Started](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/getting-started): Build your first bot, QR vs pairing login
- [Configuration](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/configuration): Full ClientOptions reference and defaults
- [Client & Lifecycle](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/client): Connect, mutations (incl. pin/unpin/setDisappearing), domain modules
- [Events](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/events): Event catalog, the `message` umbrella, and the message context object
- [Sending Messages](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/sending-messages): Every message type with the send builder
- [Media Processing](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/media): Convert/resize/transcode media, plus the incoming `ctx.media` variants
- [Interactive Messages](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/interactive): Buttons, lists, carousels, templates, CTAs
- [Rich Responses (AIRich)](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/rich-responses): Markdown-to-rich WhatsApp messages
- [Commands](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/commands): Command router, aliases, args, middleware
- [Broadcast & Schedule](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/automation): Rate-limited broadcast and scheduling
- [Profile](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/profile): Set your name, status, and avatar
- [Presence](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/presence): Online, typing, and recording indicators
- [Chats](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/chat): Archive, pin, mute, mark read, star, delete, clear
- [Contacts](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/contacts): Check who is on WhatsApp, save, and remove contacts
- [Privacy & Blocking](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/privacy): Privacy settings and block list
- [Groups](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/groups): Create/manage groups, invites, join requests
- [Communities](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/community): Communities and linked groups
- [Newsletters (Channels)](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/newsletter): Channel admin, reactions, subscribers
- [Business & Catalog](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/business): Business profile, catalog, collections, products
- [Storage Adapters](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/storage): File, Memory, SQLite, Postgres, Redis, Convex
- [Auto-Delete](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/auto-delete): Rolling-window cleanup of old messages from the local store (on by default, 1-month retention)
- [Plugins](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/plugins): Folder-based plugins with hot-reload, `definePlugin`, and auto-tracked teardown
- [Utilities](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/utilities): Exported JID/id/text/media helper functions
- [Error Handling](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/error-handling): Error classes, codes, and disconnect reasons
- [Runtime Support](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/runtimes): Node.js, Bun, Deno, Termux specifics
- [Troubleshooting & FAQ](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/troubleshooting): Common issues and fixes
- [API Reference](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/api-reference): Every public export grouped by area

## Optional

- [Full docs as one file](https://zeative.github.io/zaileys/llms-full.txt): All documentation concatenated for ingestion
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/zeative/zaileys): Source code, issues, examples
- [npm package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/zaileys): Releases and install
